summary: Tested the new self-serve tenant registration feature (POST /api/auth/register-tenant + updated Register.js). Created /app/backend/tests/test_register_tenant.py with 5 tests — all passed. Frontend Playwright flow (register -> dashboard -> super admin sees new tenant) also passed end-to-end.
passed_tests: POST /api/auth/register-tenant with unique email+subdomain returns 200, role=tenant_admin, tenant_id present, access_token cookie set
POST /api/auth/register-tenant with duplicate email returns 400 'Email already registered'
POST /api/auth/register-tenant with duplicate subdomain returns 400 'Subdomain already taken'
After register-tenant, super admin GET /api/tenants shows the new tenant with correct business_name+subdomain (no _id leak)
New user login -> GET /api/auth/me returns role=tenant_admin with correct tenant_id
Frontend /register: all fields visible (name, email, password, business_name, subdomain) with proper data-testid selectors
Frontend subdomain input: typing 'UI_REG!!' gets sanitized to 'uireg' (lowercase + strips invalid chars)
Frontend form submit -> auto-redirects to /dashboard on success
Frontend: super admin logs in, navigates to Tenants tab, new tenant (business_name + subdomain) is visible in the list
critical_code_review_comments: routes/auth.py register_tenant: if the user insert fails after tenant insert, the tenant is orphaned (no transaction/rollback). Low severity since email uniqueness is checked first, but worth noting.
Subdomain normalization happens server-side (.lower().strip()) which is good. Frontend also sanitizes client-side. No conflict.
No password length validation on backend for register-tenant (frontend enforces minLength=6 only). Consider adding Pydantic validator for consistency with UX.
No subdomain format validation on backend (e.g., reject 'admin', 'api', empty, or characters like '.'). Frontend strips to [a-z0-9-] but backend trusts client input. Consider a backend validator.
seed_data_creation: Tests create tenants with subdomain 'testreg<ts>' and users 'test_tenant_reg_<ts>@example.com'; fixture auto-cleans before+after. UI test also cleaned up post-run (ui_reg_* users and uireg* tenants).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Register-tenant feature fully working end-to-end. Pytest: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_register_tenant.py -v. The broader regression suite at backend/tests/backend_test.py (24 tests) was not re-run this iteration — no changes to those routes were made. Iteration_4 minor item (/api/auth/me returns '_id' instead of 'id') is still open but low severity.
rca of the issue: Original bug: /register page was creating a user with role=customer only and never creating a tenant, so super admin never saw the new signup in Tenants. Fix verified: new /api/auth/register-tenant endpoint now (1) inserts tenants document with provided business_name+subdomain, (2) inserts users document with role=tenant_admin and tenant_id linking to the new tenant, (3) sets auth cookies. Register.js now collects business_name + subdomain and calls the new endpoint. Duplicate email and duplicate subdomain both return 400 with appropriate messages.
